method: 采用分离视觉变换器(SepViT)增强全局特征提取，设计轻量双向特征金字塔网络(Light-BiFPN)优化特征融合，引入全局注意力机制(GAM)提升检测精度，并结合Mish激活函数与SIoU损失函数加速收敛。
result: 相较原始算法，mAP提升3.3%，参数量减少27.1%，FLOPs降低19.1%，检测速度达91.1 FPS，满足复杂火场实时检测需求。
conclusion: Light-YOLOv5通过多模块优化在保持高精度的同时显著降低计算量，为复杂火场场景的实时火情检测提供了高效解决方案。
search_text: Light-YOLOv5: A Lightweight Algorithm for Improved YOLOv5 in Complex Fire Scenarios Light-YOLOv5：复杂火灾场景下改进YOLOv5的轻量级算法 Fire-detection technology is of great importance for successful fire-prevention measures. Image-based fire detection is one effective method. At present, object-detection algorithms are deficient in performing detection speed and accuracy tasks when they are applied in complex fire scenarios. In this study, a lightweight fire-detection algorithm, Light-YOLOv5 (You Only Look Once version five), is presented. First, a separable vision transformer (SepViT) block is used to replace several C3 modules in the final layer of a backbone network to enhance both the contact of the backbone network to global in-formation and the extraction of flame and smoke features; second, a light bidirectional feature pyramid network (Light-BiFPN) is designed to lighten the model while improving the feature extraction and balancing speed and accuracy features during a fire-detection procedure; third, a global attention mechanism (GAM) is fused into the network to cause the model to focus more on the global dimensional features and further improve the detection accuracy of the model; and finally, the Mish activation function and SIoU loss are utilized to simultaneously increase the convergence speed and enhance the accuracy. The experimental results show that compared to the original algorithm, the mean average accuracy (mAP) of Light-YOLOv5 increases by 3.3%, the number of parameters decreases by 27.1%, and the floating point operations (FLOPs) decrease by 19.1%. The detection speed reaches 91.1 FPS, which can detect targets in complex fire scenarios in real time. 火灾探测技术对于成功的防火措施至关重要。基于图像的火灾探测是一种有效的方法。目前，目标检测算法在复杂火灾场景中执行检测速度和准确性方面存在不足。在这项研究中，提出了一种轻量级火灾检测算法 Light-YOLOv5（You Only Look Once version 5）。首先，使用可分离视觉变换器（SepViT）块替换主干网络最后一层中的多个C3模块，以增强主干网络与全局信息的联系以及火焰和烟雾特征的提取；其次，设计了轻型双向特征金字塔网络（Light-BiFPN），以减轻模型重量，同时改进特征提取并平衡火灾探测过程中的速度和准确性特征；第三，网络中融合全局注意力机制（GAM），使模型更加关注全局维度特征，进一步提高模型的检测精度；最后，利用Mish激活函数和SIoU损失来同时提高收敛速度和提高精度。实验结果表明，与原算法相比，Light-YOLOv5的平均准确率（mAP）提高了3.3%，参数数量减少了27.1%，浮点运算（FLOPs）减少了19.1%。检测速度达到91.1 FPS，可实时检测复杂火灾场景中的目标。 本文提出轻量级火情检测算法Light-YOLOv5，通过改进YOLOv5模型以提升复杂火场场景下的检测性能。 Hao Xu,Bo Li,Fei Zhong cs.CV

authors_text: Jiehui Zhou,Xumeng Wang,Jason K. Wong,Huanliang Wang,Zhongwei Wang,Xiaoyu Yang,Xiaoran Yan,Haozhe Feng,Huamin Qu,Haochao Ying,Wei Chen
title: DPVisCreator: Incorporating Pattern Constraints to Privacy-preserving Visualizations via Differential Privacy
title_zh: DPVisCreator：通过差异隐私将模式约束纳入隐私保护可视化
abstract: Data privacy is an essential issue in publishing data visualizations. However, it is challenging to represent multiple data patterns in privacy-preserving visualizations. The prior approaches target specific chart types or perform an anonymization model uniformly without considering the importance of data patterns in visualizations. In this paper, we propose a visual analytics approach that facilitates data custodians to generate multiple private charts while maintaining user-preferred patterns. To this end, we introduce pattern constraints to model users' preferences over data patterns in the dataset and incorporate them into the proposed Bayesian network-based Differential Privacy (DP) model PriVis. A prototype system, DPVisCreator, is developed to assist data custodians in implementing our approach. The effectiveness of our approach is demonstrated with quantitative evaluation of pattern utility under the different levels of privacy protection, case studies, and semi-structured expert interviews.
summary: Data privacy is an essential issue in publishing data visualizations. However, it is challenging to represent multiple data patterns in privacy-preserving visualizations. The prior approaches target specific chart types or perform an anonymization model uniformly without considering the importance of data patterns in visualizations. In this paper, we propose a visual analytics approach that facilitates data custodians to generate multiple private charts while maintaining user-preferred patterns. To this end, we introduce pattern constraints to model users' preferences over data patterns in the dataset and incorporate them into the proposed Bayesian network-based Differential Privacy (DP) model PriVis. A prototype system, DPVisCreator, is developed to assist data custodians in implementing our approach. The effectiveness of our approach is demonstrated with quantitative evaluation of pattern utility under the different levels of privacy protection, case studies, and semi-structured expert interviews.
abstract_zh: 数据隐私是发布数据可视化的一个重要问题。然而，在隐私保护可视化中表示多种数据模式具有挑战性。现有方法针对特定图表类型或统一执行匿名化模型，而没有考虑可视化中数据模式的重要性。在本文中，我们提出了一种可视化分析方法，可以帮助数据管理员生成多个私有图表，同时维护用户偏好的模式。为此，我们引入模式约束来模拟用户对数据集中数据模式的偏好，并将它们合并到提出的基于贝叶斯网络的差分隐私（DP）模型 PriVis 中。开发了一个原型系统 DPVisCreator，以帮助数据管理员实施我们的方法。我们的方法的有效性通过不同级别的隐私保护、案例研究和半结构化专家访谈下模式效用的定量评估来证明。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于差分隐私和模式约束的可视化方法，以在保护隐私的同时保留数据模式。
AI.motivation: 现有隐私保护可视化方法无法有效平衡隐私保护与数据模式保留，缺乏对用户偏好模式的考虑。
AI.method: 提出基于贝叶斯网络的差分隐私模型PriVis，结合用户定义的模式约束，并开发原型系统DPVisCreator实现该方法。
AI.result: 通过定量评估、案例研究和专家访谈验证了方法在不同隐私保护级别下的模式效用和有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效平衡了隐私保护与数据模式保留，为隐私保护可视化提供了新思路。

title: Cooperative coevolutionary hybrid NSGA-II with Linkage Measurement Minimization for Large-scale Multi-objective optimization
title_zh: 具有关联测量最小化的协作协同进化混合 NSGA-II 用于大规模多目标优化
abstract: In this paper, we propose a variable grouping method based on cooperative coevolution for large-scale multi-objective problems (LSMOPs), named Linkage Measurement Minimization (LMM). And for the sub-problem optimization stage, a hybrid NSGA-II with a Gaussian sampling operator based on an estimated convergence point is proposed. In the variable grouping stage, according to our previous research, we treat the variable grouping problem as a combinatorial optimization problem, and the linkage measurement function is designed based on linkage identification by the nonlinearity check on real code (LINC-R). We extend this variable grouping method to LSMOPs. In the sub-problem optimization stage, we hypothesize that there is a higher probability of existing better solutions around the Pareto Front (PF). Based on this hypothesis, we estimate a convergence point at every generation of optimization and perform Gaussian sampling around the convergence point. The samples with good objective value will participate in the optimization as elites. Numerical experiments show that our variable grouping method is better than some popular variable grouping methods, and hybrid NSGA-II has broad prospects for multi-objective problem optimization.
summary: In this paper, we propose a variable grouping method based on cooperative coevolution for large-scale multi-objective problems (LSMOPs), named Linkage Measurement Minimization (LMM). And for the sub-problem optimization stage, a hybrid NSGA-II with a Gaussian sampling operator based on an estimated convergence point is proposed. In the variable grouping stage, according to our previous research, we treat the variable grouping problem as a combinatorial optimization problem, and the linkage measurement function is designed based on linkage identification by the nonlinearity check on real code (LINC-R). We extend this variable grouping method to LSMOPs. In the sub-problem optimization stage, we hypothesize that there is a higher probability of existing better solutions around the Pareto Front (PF). Based on this hypothesis, we estimate a convergence point at every generation of optimization and perform Gaussian sampling around the convergence point. The samples with good objective value will participate in the optimization as elites. Numerical experiments show that our variable grouping method is better than some popular variable grouping methods, and hybrid NSGA-II has broad prospects for multi-objective problem optimization.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种基于协作协同进化的大规模多目标问题（LSMOP）的变量分组方法，称为链接测量最小化（LMM）。对于子问题优化阶段，提出了一种基于估计收敛点的带有高斯采样算子的混合 NSGA-II。在变量分组阶段，根据我们前期的研究，我们将变量分组问题视为组合优化问题，并基于真实代码非线性检查（LINC-R）的联动识别设计联动测量函数。我们将这种变量分组方法扩展到 LSMOP。在子问题优化阶段，我们假设帕累托前沿（PF）周围存在更好的解决方案的可能性更高。基于这个假设，我们在每一代优化时估计一个收敛点，并在收敛点周围执行高斯采样。具有良好客观价值的样本将作为精英参与优化。数值实验表明，我们的变量分组方法优于一些流行的变量分组方法，并且混合NSGA-II在多目标问题优化方面具有广阔的前景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于协同进化的变量分组方法Linkage Measurement Minimization（LMM）及混合NSGA-II算法，用于解决大规模多目标优化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理大规模多目标优化问题时存在变量分组效率低、收敛速度慢等不足，需改进算法性能。
AI.method: 变量分组阶段基于LINC-R非线性检查设计链联系数函数，子问题优化阶段采用结合高斯采样算子的混合NSGA-II，通过估计收敛点进行采样。
AI.result: 数值实验表明，LMM分组方法优于主流分组策略，混合NSGA-II在多目标优化中展现良好应用前景。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了大规模多目标优化的求解效率，为相关领域提供了新的技术思路。

authors_text: Peng Wu,Lipeng Gu,Xuefeng Yan,Haoran Xie,Fu Lee Wang,Gary Cheng,Mingqiang Wei
title: PV-RCNN++: Semantical Point-Voxel Feature Interaction for 3D Object Detection
title_zh: PV-RCNN++：用于 3D 对象检测的语义点-体素特征交互
abstract: Large imbalance often exists between the foreground points (i.e., objects) and the background points in outdoor LiDAR point clouds. It hinders cutting-edge detectors from focusing on informative areas to produce accurate 3D object detection results. This paper proposes a novel object detection network by semantical point-voxel feature interaction, dubbed PV-RCNN++. Unlike most of existing methods, PV-RCNN++ explores the semantic information to enhance the quality of object detection. First, a semantic segmentation module is proposed to retain more discriminative foreground keypoints. Such a module will guide our PV-RCNN++ to integrate more object-related point-wise and voxel-wise features in the pivotal areas. Then, to make points and voxels interact efficiently, we utilize voxel query based on Manhattan distance to quickly sample voxel-wise features around keypoints. Such the voxel query will reduce the time complexity from O(N) to O(K), compared to the ball query. Further, to avoid being stuck in learning only local features, an attention-based residual PointNet module is designed to expand the receptive field to adaptively aggregate the neighboring voxel-wise features into keypoints. Extensive experiments on the KITTI dataset show that PV-RCNN++ achieves 81.60$\%$, 40.18$\%$, 68.21$\%$ 3D mAP on Car, Pedestrian, and Cyclist, achieving comparable or even better performance to the state-of-the-arts.
summary: Large imbalance often exists between the foreground points (i.e., objects) and the background points in outdoor LiDAR point clouds. It hinders cutting-edge detectors from focusing on informative areas to produce accurate 3D object detection results. This paper proposes a novel object detection network by semantical point-voxel feature interaction, dubbed PV-RCNN++. Unlike most of existing methods, PV-RCNN++ explores the semantic information to enhance the quality of object detection. First, a semantic segmentation module is proposed to retain more discriminative foreground keypoints. Such a module will guide our PV-RCNN++ to integrate more object-related point-wise and voxel-wise features in the pivotal areas. Then, to make points and voxels interact efficiently, we utilize voxel query based on Manhattan distance to quickly sample voxel-wise features around keypoints. Such the voxel query will reduce the time complexity from O(N) to O(K), compared to the ball query. Further, to avoid being stuck in learning only local features, an attention-based residual PointNet module is designed to expand the receptive field to adaptively aggregate the neighboring voxel-wise features into keypoints. Extensive experiments on the KITTI dataset show that PV-RCNN++ achieves 81.60$\%$, 40.18$\%$, 68.21$\%$ 3D mAP on Car, Pedestrian, and Cyclist, achieving comparable or even better performance to the state-of-the-arts.
abstract_zh: 室外激光雷达点云中的前景点（即物体）和背景点之间经常存在较大的不平衡。它阻碍尖端探测器专注于信息区域以产生准确的 3D 物体检测结果。本文提出了一种通过语义点-体素特征交互的新型目标检测网络，称为 PV-RCNN++。与大多数现有方法不同，PV-RCNN++ 探索语义信息以提高目标检测的质量。首先，提出了语义分割模块来保留更具辨别力的前景关键点。这样的模块将指导我们的PV-RCNN++在关键区域集成更多与对象相关的点式和体素式特征。然后，为了使点和体素有效地交互，我们利用基于曼哈顿距离的体素查询来快速采样关键点周围的体素特征。与球查询相比，这样的体素查询将时间复杂度从 O(N) 降低到 O(K)。此外，为了避免陷入仅学习局部特征的困境，设计了基于注意力的残差 PointNet 模块来扩展感受野，以自适应地将相邻体素特征聚合为关键点。在 KITTI 数据集上进行的大量实验表明，PV-RCNN++ 在汽车、行人和骑车人上实现了 81.60$\%$、40.18$\%$、68.21$\%$ 3D mAP，实现了与最先进技术相当甚至更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出PV-RCNN++网络，通过语义点体素特征交互提升三维目标检测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有检测器因前景与背景点不平衡，难以聚焦关键区域，需利用语义信息提升检测质量。
AI.method: 提出语义分割模块筛选关键点，利用曼哈顿距离体素查询降低复杂度，并设计注意力PointNet模块聚合邻域特征。
AI.result: 在KITTI数据集上，PV-RCNN++在Car、Pedestrian、Cyclist的3D mAP分别为81.60%、40.18%、68.21%，性能优于或接近现有最佳方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过语义引导的特征交互和高效模块设计，PV-RCNN++显著提升了三维目标检测的准确性和效率，验证了方法的有效性。

search_text: PV-RCNN++: Semantical Point-Voxel Feature Interaction for 3D Object Detection PV-RCNN++：用于 3D 对象检测的语义点-体素特征交互 Large imbalance often exists between the foreground points (i.e., objects) and the background points in outdoor LiDAR point clouds. It hinders cutting-edge detectors from focusing on informative areas to produce accurate 3D object detection results. This paper proposes a novel object detection network by semantical point-voxel feature interaction, dubbed PV-RCNN++. Unlike most of existing methods, PV-RCNN++ explores the semantic information to enhance the quality of object detection. First, a semantic segmentation module is proposed to retain more discriminative foreground keypoints. Such a module will guide our PV-RCNN++ to integrate more object-related point-wise and voxel-wise features in the pivotal areas. Then, to make points and voxels interact efficiently, we utilize voxel query based on Manhattan distance to quickly sample voxel-wise features around keypoints. Such the voxel query will reduce the time complexity from O(N) to O(K), compared to the ball query. Further, to avoid being stuck in learning only local features, an attention-based residual PointNet module is designed to expand the receptive field to adaptively aggregate the neighboring voxel-wise features into keypoints. Extensive experiments on the KITTI dataset show that PV-RCNN++ achieves 81.60$\%$, 40.18$\%$, 68.21$\%$ 3D mAP on Car, Pedestrian, and Cyclist, achieving comparable or even better performance to the state-of-the-arts. 室外激光雷达点云中的前景点（即物体）和背景点之间经常存在较大的不平衡。它阻碍尖端探测器专注于信息区域以产生准确的 3D 物体检测结果。本文提出了一种通过语义点-体素特征交互的新型目标检测网络，称为 PV-RCNN++。与大多数现有方法不同，PV-RCNN++ 探索语义信息以提高目标检测的质量。首先，提出了语义分割模块来保留更具辨别力的前景关键点。这样的模块将指导我们的PV-RCNN++在关键区域集成更多与对象相关的点式和体素式特征。然后，为了使点和体素有效地交互，我们利用基于曼哈顿距离的体素查询来快速采样关键点周围的体素特征。与球查询相比，这样的体素查询将时间复杂度从 O(N) 降低到 O(K)。此外，为了避免陷入仅学习局部特征的困境，设计了基于注意力的残差 PointNet 模块来扩展感受野，以自适应地将相邻体素特征聚合为关键点。在 KITTI 数据集上进行的大量实验表明，PV-RCNN++ 在汽车、行人和骑车人上实现了 81.60$\%$、40.18$\%$、68.21$\%$ 3D mAP，实现了与最先进技术相当甚至更好的性能。 提出PV-RCNN++网络，通过语义点体素特征交互提升三维目标检测的准确性。 Peng Wu,Lipeng Gu,Xuefeng Yan,Haoran Xie,Fu Lee Wang,Gary Cheng,Mingqiang Wei cs.CV

authors_text: Junjie Hu,Chenyou Fan,Mete Ozay,Hua Feng,Yuan Gao,Tin Lun Lam
title: Progressive Self-Distillation for Ground-to-Aerial Perception Knowledge Transfer
title_zh: 用于地空感知知识转移的渐进式自蒸馏
abstract: We study a practical yet hasn't been explored problem: how a drone can perceive in an environment from different flight heights. Unlike autonomous driving, where the perception is always conducted from a ground viewpoint, a flying drone may flexibly change its flight height due to specific tasks, requiring the capability for viewpoint invariant perception. Tackling the such problem with supervised learning will incur tremendous costs for data annotation of different flying heights. On the other hand, current semi-supervised learning methods are not effective under viewpoint differences. In this paper, we introduce the ground-to-aerial perception knowledge transfer and propose a progressive semi-supervised learning framework that enables drone perception using only labeled data of ground viewpoint and unlabeled data of flying viewpoints. Our framework has four core components: i) a dense viewpoint sampling strategy that splits the range of vertical flight height into a set of small pieces with evenly-distributed intervals, ii) nearest neighbor pseudo-labeling that infers labels of the nearest neighbor viewpoint with a model learned on the preceding viewpoint, iii) MixView that generates augmented images among different viewpoints to alleviate viewpoint differences, and iv) a progressive distillation strategy to gradually learn until reaching the maximum flying height. We collect a synthesized and a real-world dataset, and we perform extensive experimental analyses to show that our method yields 22.2% and 16.9% accuracy improvement for the synthesized dataset and the real world. Code and datasets are available on https://github.com/FreeformRobotics/Progressive-Self-Distillation-for-Ground-to-Aerial-Perception-Knowledge-Transfer.
summary: We study a practical yet hasn't been explored problem: how a drone can perceive in an environment from different flight heights. Unlike autonomous driving, where the perception is always conducted from a ground viewpoint, a flying drone may flexibly change its flight height due to specific tasks, requiring the capability for viewpoint invariant perception. Tackling the such problem with supervised learning will incur tremendous costs for data annotation of different flying heights. On the other hand, current semi-supervised learning methods are not effective under viewpoint differences. In this paper, we introduce the ground-to-aerial perception knowledge transfer and propose a progressive semi-supervised learning framework that enables drone perception using only labeled data of ground viewpoint and unlabeled data of flying viewpoints. Our framework has four core components: i) a dense viewpoint sampling strategy that splits the range of vertical flight height into a set of small pieces with evenly-distributed intervals, ii) nearest neighbor pseudo-labeling that infers labels of the nearest neighbor viewpoint with a model learned on the preceding viewpoint, iii) MixView that generates augmented images among different viewpoints to alleviate viewpoint differences, and iv) a progressive distillation strategy to gradually learn until reaching the maximum flying height. We collect a synthesized and a real-world dataset, and we perform extensive experimental analyses to show that our method yields 22.2% and 16.9% accuracy improvement for the synthesized dataset and the real world. Code and datasets are available on https://github.com/FreeformRobotics/Progressive-Self-Distillation-for-Ground-to-Aerial-Perception-Knowledge-Transfer.
abstract_zh: 我们研究一个尚未探索的实际问题：无人机如何从不同的飞行高度感知环境。与自动驾驶总是从地面视点进行感知不同，飞行无人机可能会因特定任务而灵活改变飞行高度，需要具备视点不变感知能力。通过监督学习来解决此类问题将为不同飞行高度的数据注释带来巨大的成本。另一方面，当前的半监督学习方法在观点差异下并不有效。在本文中，我们介绍了地对空感知知识转移，并提出了一种渐进式半监督学习框架，该框架仅使用地面视点的标记数据和飞行视点的未标记数据来实现无人机感知。我们的框架有四个核心组件：i）密集视点采样策略，将垂直飞行高度范围分割成一组间隔均匀的小块；ii）最近邻伪标签，通过在前一个视点上学习的模型推断最近邻视点的标签；iii）MixView，在不同视点之间生成增强图像以减轻视点差异；iv）渐进式蒸馏策略，逐步学习直至达到最大飞行高度。我们收集了合成数据集和真实世界的数据集，并进行了广泛的实验分析，结果表明我们的方法对合成数据集和真实世界的准确率分别提高了 22.2% 和 16.9%。代码和数据集可在 https://github.com/FreeformRobotics/Progressive-Self-Distillation-for-Ground-to-Aerial-Perception-Knowledge-Transfer 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种渐进式自蒸馏框架，用于实现从地面视角到空中视角的感知知识迁移，使无人机能够在不同飞行高度下进行有效环境感知。
AI.motivation: 无人机在不同飞行高度下的感知任务需要标注大量多视角数据，但传统监督学习成本高昂，现有半监督方法难以应对视角差异问题。
AI.method: 框架包含四个核心组件：密集视角采样策略、最近邻伪标签生成、跨视角图像增强（MixView）以及渐进式蒸馏策略，通过逐步学习不同高度视角实现知识迁移。
AI.result: 在合成数据集和真实世界数据集上分别实现了22.2%和16.9%的准确率提升。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了视角差异导致的感知性能下降问题，为无人机多高度感知任务提供了低成本的解决方案。

title: Towards Explaining Demographic Bias through the Eyes of Face Recognition Models
title_zh: 通过人脸识别模型解释人口统计偏差
comment: Accepted at at the 2022 International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB 2022)
comments: Accepted at at the 2022 International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB 2022)
abstract: Biases inherent in both data and algorithms make the fairness of widespread machine learning (ML)-based decision-making systems less than optimal. To improve the trustfulness of such ML decision systems, it is crucial to be aware of the inherent biases in these solutions and to make them more transparent to the public and developers. In this work, we aim at providing a set of explainability tool that analyse the difference in the face recognition models' behaviors when processing different demographic groups. We do that by leveraging higher-order statistical information based on activation maps to build explainability tools that link the FR models' behavior differences to certain facial regions. The experimental results on two datasets and two face recognition models pointed out certain areas of the face where the FR models react differently for certain demographic groups compared to reference groups. The outcome of these analyses interestingly aligns well with the results of studies that analyzed the anthropometric differences and the human judgment differences on the faces of different demographic groups. This is thus the first study that specifically tries to explain the biased behavior of FR models on different demographic groups and link it directly to the spatial facial features. The code is publicly available here.
summary: Biases inherent in both data and algorithms make the fairness of widespread machine learning (ML)-based decision-making systems less than optimal. To improve the trustfulness of such ML decision systems, it is crucial to be aware of the inherent biases in these solutions and to make them more transparent to the public and developers. In this work, we aim at providing a set of explainability tool that analyse the difference in the face recognition models' behaviors when processing different demographic groups. We do that by leveraging higher-order statistical information based on activation maps to build explainability tools that link the FR models' behavior differences to certain facial regions. The experimental results on two datasets and two face recognition models pointed out certain areas of the face where the FR models react differently for certain demographic groups compared to reference groups. The outcome of these analyses interestingly aligns well with the results of studies that analyzed the anthropometric differences and the human judgment differences on the faces of different demographic groups. This is thus the first study that specifically tries to explain the biased behavior of FR models on different demographic groups and link it directly to the spatial facial features. The code is publicly available here.
abstract_zh: 数据和算法固有的偏差使得广泛使用的基于机器学习 (ML) 的决策系统的公平性不太理想。为了提高此类机器学习决策系统的可信度，了解这些解决方案中固有的偏见并使它们对公众和开发人员更加透明至关重要。在这项工作中，我们的目标是提供一套可解释性工具，用于分析人脸识别模型在处理不同人口群体时行为的差异。为此，我们利用基于激活图的高阶统计信息来构建可解释性工具，将 FR 模型的行为差异与某些面部区域联系起来。两个数据集和两个人脸识别模型的实验结果指出，与参考组相比，FR 模型对某些人口群体的反应不同。有趣的是，这些分析的结果与分析不同人口群体面部的人体测量差异和人类判断差异的研究结果非常吻合。因此，这是第一项专门尝试解释 FR 模型对不同人口群体的偏见行为并将其直接与空间面部特征联系起来的研究。该代码可在此处公开获取。

AI.tldr: 开发可解释性工具分析人脸识别模型在不同群体间的偏差，并将其与面部特征联系起来。
AI.motivation: 现有机器学习系统存在数据与算法固有偏差，影响公平性，需通过提高透明度增强公众与开发者信任。
AI.method: 基于激活图的高阶统计信息构建解释工具，定位人脸识别模型在不同群体间行为差异的面部区域。
AI.result: 实验发现模型对特定面部区域在不同群体间反应差异，结果与人类判断及人类面部测量学研究一致。
AI.conclusion: 首次将人脸识别模型的群体偏差直接关联至空间面部特征，为改进算法公平性提供依据。

search_text: Towards Explaining Demographic Bias through the Eyes of Face Recognition Models 通过人脸识别模型解释人口统计偏差 Biases inherent in both data and algorithms make the fairness of widespread machine learning (ML)-based decision-making systems less than optimal. To improve the trustfulness of such ML decision systems, it is crucial to be aware of the inherent biases in these solutions and to make them more transparent to the public and developers. In this work, we aim at providing a set of explainability tool that analyse the difference in the face recognition models' behaviors when processing different demographic groups. We do that by leveraging higher-order statistical information based on activation maps to build explainability tools that link the FR models' behavior differences to certain facial regions. The experimental results on two datasets and two face recognition models pointed out certain areas of the face where the FR models react differently for certain demographic groups compared to reference groups. The outcome of these analyses interestingly aligns well with the results of studies that analyzed the anthropometric differences and the human judgment differences on the faces of different demographic groups. This is thus the first study that specifically tries to explain the biased behavior of FR models on different demographic groups and link it directly to the spatial facial features. The code is publicly available here. 数据和算法固有的偏差使得广泛使用的基于机器学习 (ML) 的决策系统的公平性不太理想。为了提高此类机器学习决策系统的可信度，了解这些解决方案中固有的偏见并使它们对公众和开发人员更加透明至关重要。在这项工作中，我们的目标是提供一套可解释性工具，用于分析人脸识别模型在处理不同人口群体时行为的差异。为此，我们利用基于激活图的高阶统计信息来构建可解释性工具，将 FR 模型的行为差异与某些面部区域联系起来。两个数据集和两个人脸识别模型的实验结果指出，与参考组相比，FR 模型对某些人口群体的反应不同。有趣的是，这些分析的结果与分析不同人口群体面部的人体测量差异和人类判断差异的研究结果非常吻合。因此，这是第一项专门尝试解释 FR 模型对不同人口群体的偏见行为并将其直接与空间面部特征联系起来的研究。该代码可在此处公开获取。 开发可解释性工具分析人脸识别模型在不同群体间的偏差，并将其与面部特征联系起来。 Biying Fu,Naser Damer cs.CV

title: Confidence Estimation for Object Detection in Document Images
title_zh: 文档图像中对象检测的置信度估计
abstract: Deep neural networks are becoming increasingly powerful and large and always require more labelled data to be trained. However, since annotating data is time-consuming, it is now necessary to develop systems that show good performance while learning on a limited amount of data. These data must be correctly chosen to obtain models that are still efficient. For this, the systems must be able to determine which data should be annotated to achieve the best results. In this paper, we propose four estimators to estimate the confidence of object detection predictions. The first two are based on Monte Carlo dropout, the third one on descriptive statistics and the last one on the detector posterior probabilities. In the active learning framework, the three first estimators show a significant improvement in performance for the detection of document physical pages and text lines compared to a random selection of images. We also show that the proposed estimator based on descriptive statistics can replace MC dropout, reducing the computational cost without compromising the performances.
summary: Deep neural networks are becoming increasingly powerful and large and always require more labelled data to be trained. However, since annotating data is time-consuming, it is now necessary to develop systems that show good performance while learning on a limited amount of data. These data must be correctly chosen to obtain models that are still efficient. For this, the systems must be able to determine which data should be annotated to achieve the best results. In this paper, we propose four estimators to estimate the confidence of object detection predictions. The first two are based on Monte Carlo dropout, the third one on descriptive statistics and the last one on the detector posterior probabilities. In the active learning framework, the three first estimators show a significant improvement in performance for the detection of document physical pages and text lines compared to a random selection of images. We also show that the proposed estimator based on descriptive statistics can replace MC dropout, reducing the computational cost without compromising the performances.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络变得越来越强大和庞大，并且总是需要更多标记数据来进行训练。然而，由于注释数据非常耗时，因此现在有必要开发在学习有限数据的同时表现出良好性能的系统。必须正确选择这些数据才能获得仍然有效的模型。为此，系统必须能够确定应注释哪些数据才能获得最佳结果。在本文中，我们提出了四个估计器来估计对象检测预测的置信度。前两个基于蒙特卡洛丢失，第三个基于描述性统计，最后一个基于检测器后验概率。在主动学习框架中，与随机选择的图像相比，前三个估计器在检测文档物理页面和文本行方面显示出显着的性能改进。我们还表明，所提出的基于描述性统计的估计器可以取代 MC dropout，在不影响性能的情况下降低计算成本。

AI.tldr: 本文提出四种置信度估计方法，用于改进文档图像中目标检测的主动学习框架，以减少标注数据需求。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型需要大量标注数据，但人工标注耗时耗力，需开发在有限数据下仍能高效学习的系统。
AI.method: 设计基于蒙特卡洛丢弃法、描述性统计和检测器后验概率的四种置信度估计器，并在主动学习框架中验证其效果。
AI.result: 前三种估计器在文档页面和文本行检测中显著优于随机选择，描述性统计估计器可替代蒙特卡洛丢弃法，降低计算成本。
AI.conclusion: 描述性统计估计器在保持性能的同时降低了计算成本，为文档图像目标检测提供了高效解决方案。

title: Unified Bayesian Frameworks for Multi-criteria Decision-making Problems
title_zh: 多标准决策问题的统一贝叶斯框架
comment: we discovered critical issues that required us to completely restructure and rewrite the manuscript. The revisions affect every part of the paper--from the title and abstract to the core contributions and results. Given the extent of these changes, we believe it is more appropriate to withdraw the current version and submit the revised work as a new submission
comments: we discovered critical issues that required us to completely restructure and rewrite the manuscript. The revisions affect every part of the paper--from the title and abstract to the core contributions and results. Given the extent of these changes, we believe it is more appropriate to withdraw the current version and submit the revised work as a new submission
abstract: This paper introduces Bayesian frameworks for tackling various aspects of multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) problems, leveraging a probabilistic interpretation of MCDM methods and challenges. By harnessing the flexibility of Bayesian models, the proposed frameworks offer statistically elegant solutions to key challenges in MCDM, such as group decision-making problems and criteria correlation. Additionally, these models can accommodate diverse forms of uncertainty in decision makers' (DMs) preferences, including normal and triangular distributions, as well as interval preferences. To address large-scale group MCDM scenarios, a probabilistic mixture model is developed, enabling the identification of homogeneous subgroups of DMs. Furthermore, a probabilistic ranking scheme is devised to assess the relative importance of criteria and alternatives based on DM(s) preferences. Through experimentation on various numerical examples, the proposed frameworks are validated, demonstrating their effectiveness and highlighting their distinguishing features in comparison to alternative methods.
summary: This paper introduces Bayesian frameworks for tackling various aspects of multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) problems, leveraging a probabilistic interpretation of MCDM methods and challenges. By harnessing the flexibility of Bayesian models, the proposed frameworks offer statistically elegant solutions to key challenges in MCDM, such as group decision-making problems and criteria correlation. Additionally, these models can accommodate diverse forms of uncertainty in decision makers' (DMs) preferences, including normal and triangular distributions, as well as interval preferences. To address large-scale group MCDM scenarios, a probabilistic mixture model is developed, enabling the identification of homogeneous subgroups of DMs. Furthermore, a probabilistic ranking scheme is devised to assess the relative importance of criteria and alternatives based on DM(s) preferences. Through experimentation on various numerical examples, the proposed frameworks are validated, demonstrating their effectiveness and highlighting their distinguishing features in comparison to alternative methods.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了用于解决多标准决策 (MCDM) 问题各个方面的贝叶斯框架，利用对 MCDM 方法和挑战的概率解释。通过利用贝叶斯模型的灵活性，所提出的框架为 MCDM 中的关键挑战（例如群体决策问题和标准相关性）提供了统计上优雅的解决方案。此外，这些模型可以适应决策者 (DM) 偏好中多种形式的不确定性，包括正态分布和三角分布以及区间偏好。为了解决大规模组 MCDM 场景，开发了概率混合模型，能够识别 DM 的同质子组。此外，还设计了概率排名方案，以根据 DM 偏好评估标准和替代方案的相对重要性。通过对各种数值示例的实验，所提出的框架得到了验证，证明了它们的有效性并突出了它们与替代方法相比的显着特征。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种统一的贝叶斯框架，用于解决多准则决策问题中的关键挑战，如群体决策和准则相关性，并处理决策者偏好的不确定性。
AI.motivation: 现有多准则决策方法难以有效处理群体决策中的不确定性、准则间相关性及大规模决策者偏好差异，需更灵活的概率模型来提升决策鲁棒性。
AI.method: 构建贝叶斯框架，引入概率混合模型划分同质子群体，设计概率排序方案评估准则与方案重要性，支持正态分布、三角分布及区间偏好等多形式不确定性。
AI.result: 通过数值实验验证框架有效性，证明其在处理群体决策、准则相关性及不确定性方面优于传统方法，且能准确识别子群体并排序决策方案。
AI.conclusion: 贝叶斯框架为多准则决策提供了统计优雅的解决方案，有效应对不确定性与群体异质性，为复杂决策场景提供了灵活工具。

method: 构建贝叶斯框架，引入概率混合模型划分同质子群体，设计概率排序方案评估准则与方案重要性，支持正态分布、三角分布及区间偏好等多形式不确定性。
result: 通过数值实验验证框架有效性，证明其在处理群体决策、准则相关性及不确定性方面优于传统方法，且能准确识别子群体并排序决策方案。
conclusion: 贝叶斯框架为多准则决策提供了统计优雅的解决方案，有效应对不确定性与群体异质性，为复杂决策场景提供了灵活工具。
search_text: Unified Bayesian Frameworks for Multi-criteria Decision-making Problems 多标准决策问题的统一贝叶斯框架 This paper introduces Bayesian frameworks for tackling various aspects of multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) problems, leveraging a probabilistic interpretation of MCDM methods and challenges. By harnessing the flexibility of Bayesian models, the proposed frameworks offer statistically elegant solutions to key challenges in MCDM, such as group decision-making problems and criteria correlation. Additionally, these models can accommodate diverse forms of uncertainty in decision makers' (DMs) preferences, including normal and triangular distributions, as well as interval preferences. To address large-scale group MCDM scenarios, a probabilistic mixture model is developed, enabling the identification of homogeneous subgroups of DMs. Furthermore, a probabilistic ranking scheme is devised to assess the relative importance of criteria and alternatives based on DM(s) preferences. Through experimentation on various numerical examples, the proposed frameworks are validated, demonstrating their effectiveness and highlighting their distinguishing features in comparison to alternative methods. 本文介绍了用于解决多标准决策 (MCDM) 问题各个方面的贝叶斯框架，利用对 MCDM 方法和挑战的概率解释。通过利用贝叶斯模型的灵活性，所提出的框架为 MCDM 中的关键挑战（例如群体决策问题和标准相关性）提供了统计上优雅的解决方案。此外，这些模型可以适应决策者 (DM) 偏好中多种形式的不确定性，包括正态分布和三角分布以及区间偏好。为了解决大规模组 MCDM 场景，开发了概率混合模型，能够识别 DM 的同质子组。此外，还设计了概率排名方案，以根据 DM 偏好评估标准和替代方案的相对重要性。通过对各种数值示例的实验，所提出的框架得到了验证，证明了它们的有效性并突出了它们与替代方法相比的显着特征。 本文提出了一种统一的贝叶斯框架，用于解决多准则决策问题中的关键挑战，如群体决策和准则相关性，并处理决策者偏好的不确定性。 Majid Mohammadi cs.AI

title: Affective Manifolds: Modeling Machine's Mind to Like, Dislike, Enjoy, Suffer, Worry, Fear, and Feel Like A Human
title_zh: 情感流形：模拟机器的思维，喜欢、不喜欢、享受、痛苦、担心、恐惧和感觉像人类
abstract: After the development of different machine learning and manifold learning algorithms, it may be a good time to put them together to make a powerful mind for machine. In this work, we propose affective manifolds as components of a machine's mind. Every affective manifold models a characteristic group of mind and contains multiple states. We define the machine's mind as a set of affective manifolds. We use a learning model for mapping the input signals to the embedding space of affective manifold. Using this mapping, a machine or a robot takes an input signal and can react emotionally to it. We use deep metric learning, with Siamese network, and propose a loss function for affective manifold learning. We define margins between states based on the psychological and philosophical studies. Using triplets of instances, we train the network to minimize the variance of every state and have the desired distances between states. We show that affective manifolds can have various applications for machine-machine and human-machine interactions. Some simulations are also provided for verification of the proposed method. It is possible to have as many affective manifolds as required in machine's mind. More affective manifolds in the machine's mind can make it more realistic and effective. This paper opens the door; we invite the researchers from various fields of science to propose more affective manifolds to be inserted in machine's mind.
summary: After the development of different machine learning and manifold learning algorithms, it may be a good time to put them together to make a powerful mind for machine. In this work, we propose affective manifolds as components of a machine's mind. Every affective manifold models a characteristic group of mind and contains multiple states. We define the machine's mind as a set of affective manifolds. We use a learning model for mapping the input signals to the embedding space of affective manifold. Using this mapping, a machine or a robot takes an input signal and can react emotionally to it. We use deep metric learning, with Siamese network, and propose a loss function for affective manifold learning. We define margins between states based on the psychological and philosophical studies. Using triplets of instances, we train the network to minimize the variance of every state and have the desired distances between states. We show that affective manifolds can have various applications for machine-machine and human-machine interactions. Some simulations are also provided for verification of the proposed method. It is possible to have as many affective manifolds as required in machine's mind. More affective manifolds in the machine's mind can make it more realistic and effective. This paper opens the door; we invite the researchers from various fields of science to propose more affective manifolds to be inserted in machine's mind.
abstract_zh: 在开发出不同的机器学习和流形学习算法之后，可能是将它们组合在一起以形成强大的机器思维的好时机。在这项工作中，我们提出情感流形作为机器思维的组成部分。每个情感流形都模拟了一个特有的心理群体，并包含多种状态。我们将机器的思维定义为一组情感流形。我们使用学习模型将输入信号映射到情感流形的嵌入空间。使用这种映射，机器或机器人可以接收输入信号并对其做出情感反应。我们使用深度度量学习和 Siamese 网络，并提出了情感流形学习的损失函数。我们根据心理学和哲学研究来定义状态之间的界限。使用实例的三元组，我们训练网络以最小化每个状态的方差并具有状态之间的所需距离。我们证明情感流形可以在机器与机器和人机交互中具有多种应用。还提供了一些模拟来验证所提出的方法。机器头脑中可以拥有尽可能多的情感流形。机器头脑中更多的情感流形可以使其更加现实和有效。这篇论文打开了大门；我们邀请来自各个科学领域的研究人员提出更多情感流形以插入机器的思维。

AI.tldr: 提出情感流形模型，使机器能够模拟人类情感反应，增强人机交互的真实感。
AI.motivation: 现有算法发展成熟，需整合以创造更强大、更人性化的机器思维，提升交互真实感。
AI.method: 采用深度度量学习与Siamese网络，结合心理学理论定义状态间隔，通过三元组训练优化情感流形。
AI.result: 验证了情感流形在机器交互中的有效性，证明其可提升机器情感反应的真实性与实用性。
AI.conclusion: 情感流形模型为机器情感模拟提供新方法，鼓励跨学科合作扩展应用。

authors_text: Gregory Holste,Song Wang,Ziyu Jiang,Thomas C. Shen,George Shih,Ronald M. Summers,Yifan Peng,Zhangyang Wang
title: Long-Tailed Classification of Thorax Diseases on Chest X-Ray: A New Benchmark Study
title_zh: 胸部 X 光胸部疾病的长尾分类：一项新的基准研究
abstract: Imaging exams, such as chest radiography, will yield a small set of common findings and a much larger set of uncommon findings. While a trained radiologist can learn the visual presentation of rare conditions by studying a few representative examples, teaching a machine to learn from such a "long-tailed" distribution is much more difficult, as standard methods would be easily biased toward the most frequent classes. In this paper, we present a comprehensive benchmark study of the long-tailed learning problem in the specific domain of thorax diseases on chest X-rays. We focus on learning from naturally distributed chest X-ray data, optimizing classification accuracy over not only the common "head" classes, but also the rare yet critical "tail" classes. To accomplish this, we introduce a challenging new long-tailed chest X-ray benchmark to facilitate research on developing long-tailed learning methods for medical image classification. The benchmark consists of two chest X-ray datasets for 19- and 20-way thorax disease classification, containing classes with as many as 53,000 and as few as 7 labeled training images. We evaluate both standard and state-of-the-art long-tailed learning methods on this new benchmark, analyzing which aspects of these methods are most beneficial for long-tailed medical image classification and summarizing insights for future algorithm design. The datasets, trained models, and code are available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/LongTailCXR.
summary: Imaging exams, such as chest radiography, will yield a small set of common findings and a much larger set of uncommon findings. While a trained radiologist can learn the visual presentation of rare conditions by studying a few representative examples, teaching a machine to learn from such a "long-tailed" distribution is much more difficult, as standard methods would be easily biased toward the most frequent classes. In this paper, we present a comprehensive benchmark study of the long-tailed learning problem in the specific domain of thorax diseases on chest X-rays. We focus on learning from naturally distributed chest X-ray data, optimizing classification accuracy over not only the common "head" classes, but also the rare yet critical "tail" classes. To accomplish this, we introduce a challenging new long-tailed chest X-ray benchmark to facilitate research on developing long-tailed learning methods for medical image classification. The benchmark consists of two chest X-ray datasets for 19- and 20-way thorax disease classification, containing classes with as many as 53,000 and as few as 7 labeled training images. We evaluate both standard and state-of-the-art long-tailed learning methods on this new benchmark, analyzing which aspects of these methods are most beneficial for long-tailed medical image classification and summarizing insights for future algorithm design. The datasets, trained models, and code are available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/LongTailCXR.
abstract_zh: 影像检查（例如胸部X光检查）将产生一小部分常见发现和大量不常见发现。虽然训练有素的放射科医生可以通过研究一些有代表性的例子来学习罕见情况的视觉表现，但教机器从这种“长尾”分布中学习要困难得多，因为标准方法很容易偏向最常见的类别。在本文中，我们对胸部 X 光胸部疾病特定领域的长尾学习问题进行了全面的基准研究。我们专注于从自然分布的胸部 X 射线数据中学习，不仅优化常见“头部”类别的分类准确性，还优化罕见但关键“尾部”类别的分类准确性。为了实现这一目标，我们引入了一种具有挑战性的新型长尾胸部 X 射线基准，以促进开发用于医学图像分类的长尾学习方法的研究。该基准由用于 19 路和 20 路胸部疾病分类的两个胸部 X 射线数据集组成，其中包含多达 53,000 个和少至 7 个标记训练图像的类别。我们在此新基准上评估标准和最先进的长尾学习方法，分析这些方法的哪些方面对长尾医学图像分类最有利，并总结对未来算法设计的见解。数据集、训练模型和代码可在 https://github.com/VITA-Group/LongTailCXR 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个针对胸部X光片中胸腔疾病长尾分类问题的新基准研究，旨在解决医学图像分类中罕见疾病识别困难的问题。
AI.motivation: 医学影像中常见疾病样本多而罕见疾病样本少，导致传统分类方法偏向高频类别，难以有效识别关键但罕见的胸腔疾病。
AI.method: 构建包含19类和20类胸腔疾病的数据集（样本量从7到53,000不等），并评估标准及先进长尾学习方法在该基准上的表现。
AI.result: 分析了不同方法在长尾分布下的分类效果，总结出对罕见疾病识别有帮助的技术特点，并公开数据集与代码。
AI.conclusion: 该基准为医学图像长尾学习研究提供支持，强调需优化算法以平衡常见与罕见类别的分类性能。

The benchmark consists of two chest X-ray datasets for 19- and 20-way thorax disease classification, containing classes with as many as 53,000 and as few as 7 labeled training images. We evaluate both standard and state-of-the-art long-tailed learning methods on this new benchmark, analyzing which aspects of these methods are most beneficial for long-tailed medical image classification and summarizing insights for future algorithm design. The datasets, trained models, and code are available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/LongTailCXR. 影像检查（例如胸部X光检查）将产生一小部分常见发现和大量不常见发现。虽然训练有素的放射科医生可以通过研究一些有代表性的例子来学习罕见情况的视觉表现，但教机器从这种“长尾”分布中学习要困难得多，因为标准方法很容易偏向最常见的类别。在本文中，我们对胸部 X 光胸部疾病特定领域的长尾学习问题进行了全面的基准研究。我们专注于从自然分布的胸部 X 射线数据中学习，不仅优化常见“头部”类别的分类准确性，还优化罕见但关键“尾部”类别的分类准确性。为了实现这一目标，我们引入了一种具有挑战性的新型长尾胸部 X 射线基准，以促进开发用于医学图像分类的长尾学习方法的研究。该基准由用于 19 路和 20 路胸部疾病分类的两个胸部 X 射线数据集组成，其中包含多达 53,000 个和少至 7 个标记训练图像的类别。我们在此新基准上评估标准和最先进的长尾学习方法，分析这些方法的哪些方面对长尾医学图像分类最有利，并总结对未来算法设计的见解。数据集、训练模型和代码可在 https://github.com/VITA-Group/LongTailCXR 上获取。 本文提出一个针对胸部X光片中胸腔疾病长尾分类问题的新基准研究，旨在解决医学图像分类中罕见疾病识别困难的问题。 Gregory Holste,Song Wang,Ziyu Jiang,Thomas C. Shen,George Shih,Ronald M. Summers,Yifan Peng,Zhangyang Wang cs.CV
